title: Crepidiastrum tenuicaule
intro: Crepidiastrum tenuicaule, also known as Crepidiastrum or Korean Crepis, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Crepidiastrum tenuicaule include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional East Asian medicine, Crepidiastrum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in formulations to support digestive and respiratory health.
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum tenuicaule is a species of flowering plant in the family Tropaeolaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Crepidiastrum tenuicaule is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the order Brassicales. This plant belongs to the genus Crepidiastrum and the species C. tenuicaule. The classification helps in identifying and understanding the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
